description: Integrate authentication with WebSocket connections and game persistence. When connecting via WebSocket, authenticate with JWT token so the server knows which user is playing. Associate games with user IDs. Update the lobby system so each player in a game is linked to their user account. Update resumeGame to match by user ID instead of random player ID.
steps: In src/server/database.ts, add a 'game_players' table linking game_id to user_id with their clan selection. Add functions: addGamePlayer(gameId, userId, clanId), getGamesByUserId(userId), getGamePlayersByGameId(gameId).

Modify the WebSocket connection handler in src/server/index.ts: accept a token query parameter on WebSocket connection (ws://host:3001?token=xxx). Verify the token and associate the connection with the user. If no valid token, still allow connection but mark as anonymous (for hotseat/spectating).

Update CREATE_LOBBY and JOIN_LOBBY handlers in src/server/index.ts to use the authenticated user's ID as the player ID (instead of generating a random uuid). Store the user's username as the player name.
When a game starts (startLobbyGame), call addGamePlayer for each participant to persist the user_id to game mapping.
Add a new REST endpoint GET /api/games/my-games that requires auth and returns games where the authenticated user is a participant (using getGamesByUserId). Include whose turn it is in the response.
Update the resumeGame function in src/store/gameStore.ts to use authUser.id to identify which player the current user controls in a loaded game.
Update the WebSocket connectWebSocket function in gameStore.ts to pass the auth token as a query parameter when connecting.
In GamesLobby.tsx, fetch /api/games/my-games (with auth header) to show only the user's games, and display whose turn it is.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build succeeds without errors
WebSocket connections pass auth token and server identifies the user
Games are associated with user accounts in the database
GET /api/games/my-games returns only games the authenticated user participates in
resumeGame correctly identifies which player the user controls based on their user ID
GamesLobby shows the user's games with turn information
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm zero errors
